title: Phase 3: Snapshot & Large Page Handling
overview: Add snapshot windowing/pagination, Google SERP fast-path extraction, and DOM image extraction to browse_cli. Snapshot windowing truncates ARIA snapshots exceeding 80K chars with offset-based pagination and preserves navigation/pagination links at the tail. Google SERP fast-path uses DOM-based extraction on google.com/search pages instead of ariaSnapshot for ~2x faster structured results. The images command lists page images with metadata and optional inline base64. All three features are runtime-agnostic (work with Playwright, Camoufox, or any BrowserTarget). Phase 3 of 6 in the camoufox integration roadmap.
scopeChallenge: handleSnapshot() in src/browser/snapshot.ts returns a plain string from ariaSnapshot(). For large pages (e.g. search results, dashboards, long articles), this string can exceed 80K characters, overwhelming AI agent context windows. The windowing solution wraps the output post-render with offset-based pagination, preserving tail navigation links (~5K chars). This is a pure post-processing wrapper that does not touch the ref-map building or locator assignment — it operates on the final rendered string only. The SERP fast-path short-circuits the snapshot path entirely when the URL matches google.com/search, returning structured DOM extraction instead of ARIA tree parsing. The images command is a new read command following the existing registry pattern (register in registry.ts, implement in read.ts, wire via registerReadDefinitions).

prerequisites.assumption: handleSnapshot() returns a rendered string and stores refMap via bm.setRefMap()
prerequisites.verification: src/browser/snapshot.ts:547-549 — builds output.join('\n'), calls bm.setLastSnapshot(), returns rendered string

prerequisites.assumption: handleSnapshot is called from inspection.ts (meta command) and action-context.ts (finalize)
Command registry pattern supports adding new read commands with MCP schemas
prerequisites.verification: src/automation/registry.ts:29-127 — r() helper + registerAll() with inputSchema and argDecode

prerequisites.assumption: registerReadDefinitions() in src/commands/read.ts wires registry specs to handleReadCommand dispatch
prerequisites.verification: src/commands/read.ts:472-495 — iterates registry.byCategory('read'), defines execute functions

prerequisites.assumption: BrowserTarget interface provides getPage() for DOM evaluation in read commands
prerequisites.verification: src/browser/target.ts — BrowserTarget interface with getPage() returning Page

nonGoals: Streaming snapshot output (would require HTTP response streaming)
Server-side image compression or thumbnail generation
SERP extraction for non-Google search engines (Bing, DuckDuckGo)
Modifying the ref-map building or locator assignment logic

contracts.shape: { text: string, truncated: boolean, totalChars: number, hasMore: boolean, nextOffset: number | null } — when not truncated, text is the full snapshot unchanged
contracts.consistencyRule: Windowing is applied AFTER ref-map building and setLastSnapshot. The full snapshot is always stored for delta comparison; only the returned text is windowed. Action context callers (delta/full) may want the unwound version — windowing is opt-in via args.

contracts.shape: string — formatted SERP results with title, URL, snippet per result, plus 'People also ask' and pagination sections
contracts.consistencyRule: SERP extraction does NOT populate the refMap. It returns a plain text result. Callers that need refs (e.g. click @e3) must use the regular snapshot path. SERP is a read-only fast path for content extraction.

contracts.shape: string — one line per image: 'src alt width x height [size]', or JSON array when --json flag
contracts.consistencyRule: Images command is read-only, safe to retry, supports @ref selectors for scoping to a container element

existingCodeLeverage.existingCode: src/browser/snapshot.ts:547 — output.join('\n') is the final render point
bm.getPage().url() — available in all command contexts
src/commands/read.ts:72-78 (links command) — page.evaluate pattern for extracting DOM elements
src/automation/registry.ts:29-127 — r() helper with inputSchema and argDecode

failureModes.risk: Windowing splits a ref line mid-string — agent sees @e42 in text but ref was built from full snapshot, so click @e42 still works. However, agent may reference @e50 from metadata hint without seeing its text.
failureModes.mitigation: Return metadata (hasMore, nextOffset) so agents can request next page. Refs remain valid because refMap is built from the full snapshot before windowing. Include a hint line at truncation point: '[truncated — 120K chars total, showing 0-80K. Use snapshot --offset 80000 for next page]'

failureModes.risk: Google changes SERP DOM structure — extraction breaks silently returning empty results
failureModes.mitigation: SERP extraction falls back to regular snapshot if zero results extracted. Log a warning so the agent knows the fast path failed. Selectors use multiple fallback patterns (h3, [data-header-feature], .g class).

failureModes.risk: Action context finalizeWriteContext calls handleSnapshot for delta/full — windowing would truncate the delta baseline
failureModes.mitigation: Action context path does NOT apply windowing. The full snapshot is always stored via setLastSnapshot. Windowing is only applied in the inspection.ts snapshot command path, not in action-context.ts.

failureModes.risk: Images command on pages with hundreds of images returns very large output
failureModes.mitigation: Default limit of 100 images. Support --limit N flag. Support --selector to scope to a container. No inline base64 by default — only with --inline flag.

failureModes.risk: SERP fast-path does not populate refMap — agent tries click @e3 after SERP snapshot and gets 'ref not found'
failureModes.mitigation: SERP output clearly states it's a fast-path extraction, not a ref-mapped snapshot. Hint: 'To interact with elements, run snapshot -i instead.' Agent can fall back to full snapshot when clicks are needed.

testCoverageMap.codepath: applySnapshotWindow() truncates at 80K chars with metadata
applySnapshotWindow() returns full snapshot unchanged when under limit
applySnapshotWindow() offset-based pagination returns correct slice
extractGoogleSerp() returns structured results from Google SERP HTML
extractGoogleSerp() returns null on non-Google pages
extractGoogleSerp() falls back gracefully when selectors miss
handleSnapshot with --offset flag returns paginated output
snapshot on Google SERP page uses fast-path extraction
images command returns image metadata from page
images command with --limit flag caps output
images command with @ref scoping extracts from container only

tasks.title: Snapshot windowing module (src/browser/snapshot-window.ts)
tasks.description:
Create `src/browser/snapshot-window.ts` — a pure post-processing module that applies windowing to snapshot output strings.

Export `applySnapshotWindow(text: string, opts?: WindowOpts): WindowResult` where:
- `WindowOpts`: `{ maxChars?: number (default 80000), offset?: number (default 0), tailReserve?: number (default 5000) }`
- `WindowResult`: `{ text: string, truncated: boolean, totalChars: number, hasMore: boolean, nextOffset: number | null }`

Logic:
1. If `text.length <= maxChars`, return unchanged with `truncated: false`
2. Otherwise, find the last complete line boundary before `offset + maxChars - tailReserve`
3. Extract tail section: scan backwards from end of `text` to find navigation/pagination-related lines (lines containing 'link', 'navigation', 'pagination', 'next', 'previous', 'page'). Reserve up to `tailReserve` chars for this tail.
4. Compose: `head portion + '\n[... truncated ...]\n' + tail portion`
5. Set `nextOffset = offset + headPortion.length`, `hasMore = nextOffset < totalChars`

Also export a `formatWindowMetadata(result: WindowResult): string` helper that returns a single metadata line like `[snapshot: 120000 chars, showing 0-80000, hasMore=true, nextOffset=80000]`.

This module is pure (no Playwright imports, no side effects). It operates on the rendered string output, not on the ARIA tree or ref map.

Update `src/browser/index.ts` barrel exports to include `applySnapshotWindow` and `formatWindowMetadata`.
acceptanceCriteria: applySnapshotWindow() returns unchanged text with truncated=false when input is under 80K chars
applySnapshotWindow() truncates at line boundary and includes tail navigation section when input exceeds 80K chars
WindowResult metadata (totalChars, hasMore, nextOffset) is accurate for pagination
Offset-based pagination: applySnapshotWindow(text, { offset: 80000 }) returns the next page of content
formatWindowMetadata() returns a single-line summary of truncation state

tasks.title: Google SERP extraction module (src/browser/serp.ts)
tasks.description:
Create `src/browser/serp.ts` — a module that extracts structured search results from Google SERP pages via DOM evaluation.

Export `isGoogleSerp(url: string): boolean` — returns true if URL matches `google.com/search` or `google.XX/search` patterns.

Export `extractGoogleSerp(page: Page): Promise<string | null>` — runs `page.evaluate()` to extract:
1. Search results: each with title (from h3), URL (from a[href]), snippet (from .VwiC3b or adjacent span)
2. 'People also ask' section: questions from expandable cards
3. Pagination: next/previous page links
4. Knowledge panel: if present, title and summary text

Format output as structured text:
```
[Google Search Results]

1. Title of Result
   https://example.com/page
   Snippet text describing the result...

2. Second Result
   ...

[People Also Ask]
- Question one?
- Question two?

[Pagination]
Next: page 2
```

Return `null` if zero results extracted (triggers fallback to regular snapshot).

Use multiple fallback selectors for each element since Google frequently changes class names. The evaluate function should try selectors in order: `.g h3`, `h3[data-header-feature]`, `div[data-sokoban] h3`.

Port from camofox-browser server.js extractGoogleSerp (lines 1132-1268), adapting from Playwright-Python to Playwright-Node API (page.evaluate instead of page.query_selector_all).

Update `src/browser/index.ts` barrel exports to include `isGoogleSerp` and `extractGoogleSerp`.
acceptanceCriteria: isGoogleSerp() correctly identifies google.com/search, google.co.uk/search, google.de/search URLs
isGoogleSerp() returns false for non-Google URLs and google.com non-search pages
extractGoogleSerp() returns formatted text with numbered results including title, URL, snippet
extractGoogleSerp() returns null when no results are found (empty page, non-SERP page)
extractGoogleSerp() includes 'People also ask' section when present

tasks.title: Wire snapshot windowing into snapshot command path
tasks.description:
Integrate the snapshot windowing module into the snapshot command path. CRITICAL: windowing must NOT be applied inside handleSnapshot() itself — it must be applied at the command-handler level only. finalizeWriteContext() (action-context.ts:397) calls handleSnapshot() for delta/full context; if windowing truncated the output there, it would corrupt context deltas and full snapshots for large pages.

1. **Parse new args in parseSnapshotArgs** (`src/browser/snapshot.ts`):
   - `--offset N`: start position for windowed output (default 0)
   - `--max-chars N`: override default 80K limit
   These produce new fields on `SnapshotOptions`: `offset?: number`, `maxChars?: number`.
   handleSnapshot() returns the FULL snapshot text as before — no windowing inside.

2. **Apply windowing at the command-handler level** (`src/commands/meta/inspection.ts`):
   In the snapshot case, after calling `handleSnapshot(args, bm)`, apply `applySnapshotWindow(result, { offset: opts.offset, maxChars: opts.maxChars })` to the returned text. If truncated, append the metadata line. This is the ONLY place windowing is applied.
   The full unwindowed snapshot is already stored by handleSnapshot via `bm.setLastSnapshot(rendered, args)` and `bm.setRefMap(refMap)` — those remain unaffected.

3. **Action context path is explicitly safe**: finalizeWriteContext() calls handleSnapshot() directly and gets the full unwindowed text. No changes needed to action-context.ts. This is a hard invariant: action context must always see the complete snapshot for accurate delta computation.

4. **Update MCP schema in registry.ts**: Add `offset` and `maxChars` optional parameters to the snapshot command's MCP inputSchema.

5. **Also wire windowing in the MCP server path** (`src/mcp/server.ts`): The MCP server calls handleSnapshot and returns the result. Apply windowing after handleSnapshot returns, same as the inspection.ts path.
acceptanceCriteria: snapshot command on a large page returns truncated output with metadata line when ARIA text exceeds 80K chars
snapshot --offset 80000 returns the next page of a large snapshot
bm.getLastSnapshot() always returns the FULL unwindowed snapshot text (for delta comparison)
bm.getRefMap() contains ALL refs from the full snapshot (not just the windowed portion)
Action context (finalizeWriteContext → handleSnapshot) always receives full unwindowed snapshot — verified by checking that handleSnapshot() never calls applySnapshotWindow()

tasks.title: Wire SERP fast-path into snapshot handler
tasks.description:
Integrate the Google SERP extraction module into the snapshot command path in `src/commands/meta/inspection.ts`.

SAFETY: The SERP fast-path is OPT-IN only (default OFF). It activates when (a) `--serp` flag is passed in snapshot args, OR (b) `BROWSE_SERP_FASTPATH=1` env var is set. Without either, snapshot on Google pages uses the standard ARIA snapshot with refs — preserving backward compatibility. This prevents agents from receiving unexpected output format changes.

In `handleInspectionCommand` case `'snapshot'` (line 48-49), before calling `handleSnapshot(args, bm)`:
1. Check if SERP fast-path is enabled: args contain `--serp` OR `BROWSE_SERP_FASTPATH === '1'`
2. If enabled, get current page URL: `bm.getPage().url()`
3. Call `isGoogleSerp(url)`
4. If true AND no special flags that require refs (check: args don't contain `-C`, `--offset`), call `extractGoogleSerp(bm.getPage())`
5. If extraction returns non-null, return `'[Google SERP fast-path]\n' + result`
6. If extraction returns null (failed) or SERP not enabled, fall through to regular `handleSnapshot(args, bm)`

The SERP fast-path skips ref-map building entirely. The output clearly marks itself as `[Google SERP fast-path]` so agents know they can't use @refs.

Add `BROWSE_SERP_FASTPATH` to src/constants.ts DEFAULTS (default '0').

Update the MCP schema for snapshot in `src/automation/registry.ts` to document the SERP fast-path and add the `serp` boolean parameter.
acceptanceCriteria: snapshot on Google WITHOUT --serp flag uses standard ARIA snapshot with refs (backward compatible, default)
snapshot --serp on a Google search page returns SERP fast-path output prefixed with '[Google SERP fast-path]'
BROWSE_SERP_FASTPATH=1 enables SERP fast-path without --serp flag
Failed SERP extraction falls back to regular snapshot silently
SERP fast-path is NOT triggered during action-context delta/full (those call handleSnapshot directly, not handleInspectionCommand)

tasks.title: Add images read command (src/commands/read.ts + registry)
tasks.description: Add a new `images` read command that lists `` elements on the page with metadata. 1. **Register in `src/automation/registry.ts`**: Add a new read command registration using the `r()` helper: ``` r('images', 'List page images with metadata', { usage: '[sel] [--limit N] [--inline]', safeToRetry: true, pageContent: true, mcp: { ... } }) ``` MCP schema: optional `selector` (scope to container), optional `limit` (number, default 100), optional `inline` (boolean, include base64 data). 2. **Implement in `src/commands/read.ts`**: Add a case `'images'` to the `handleReadCommand` switch. - Parse args: optional selector (first non-flag arg or @ref), `--limit N`, `--inline` - If selector provided, use `bm.resolveRef()` for @ref support, then scope query to that container - Run `page.evaluate()` to extract all `` elements within scope: `{ src, alt, width, height, naturalWidth, naturalHeight, loading }` - Filter out images with empty src, data:image placeholders under 100 bytes, and tracking pixels (1x1) - Apply limit (default 100) - Format: one line per image — `src | alt | WxH [naturalWxH]` - With `--inline`: include base64 encoded src as additional field (only for same-origin or CORS-enabled images) - Return formatted string 3. **Update read command help** in CLI if applicable. Follow the pattern of the `links` command (line 72-78 in read.ts) for DOM evaluation, and the `count` command for @ref selector resolution.
acceptanceCriteria: browse images returns list of page images with src, alt, and dimensions
browse images --limit 5 caps output to 5 images
browse images <selector> scopes extraction to elements within container
browse images @e3 resolves @ref and scopes to that container
Tracking pixels (1x1) and empty-src images are filtered out
images command is registered in registry as a read command with MCP schema

tasks.title: Tests for snapshot windowing, SERP extraction, images command
tasks.description: Add comprehensive tests for all Phase 3 features. 1. **Snapshot windowing unit tests** (new describe block in `test/snapshot.test.ts`): - `applySnapshotWindow()` returns unchanged when input < 80K chars - `applySnapshotWindow()` truncates at line boundary when input > 80K chars - Truncated output includes tail navigation links - WindowResult metadata is accurate (totalChars, hasMore, nextOffset) - Offset pagination: second page starts where first page ended - `formatWindowMetadata()` produces correct summary line - Edge case: input exactly at 80K boundary - Edge case: no navigation links in tail 2. **SERP extraction unit tests** (new describe block in `test/snapshot.test.ts`): - `isGoogleSerp()` returns true for google.com/search, google.co.uk/search - `isGoogleSerp()` returns false for google.com/maps, google.com, bing.com/search - `extractGoogleSerp()` with mock SERP HTML fixture returns structured results - `extractGoogleSerp()` returns null on non-SERP page 3. **Snapshot windowing integration test** (in `test/snapshot.test.ts`): - Create a test fixture with a very long page (>80K chars of accessible content) - Run snapshot → verify truncation metadata - Run snapshot --offset N → verify pagination continues correctly - Verify refs from windowed snapshot are still clickable 4. **SERP integration test** (in `test/snapshot.test.ts`): - Create a test fixture that mimics Google SERP DOM structure - Navigate to fixture → run snapshot → verify SERP fast-path activates - Verify --no-serp flag bypasses fast-path 5. **Images command integration tests** (in `test/commands.test.ts`): - Create a test fixture with multiple elements (various sizes, alts, src types) - `images` returns all non-tracking images - `images --limit 2` returns only 2 - `images ` scopes correctly - 1x1 tracking pixels are filtered - Images with empty src are filtered 6. **Test fixtures** (in `test/fixtures/`): - `large-snapshot.html` — page with >80K chars of accessible content (repeated sections) - `google-serp.html` — static mock of Google SERP DOM structure - `images.html` — page with variety of images (normal, 1x1 pixel, no src, large) Register new fixtures in `test/test-server.ts`.
acceptanceCriteria: All unit tests for applySnapshotWindow() pass including edge cases
All unit tests for isGoogleSerp() and extractGoogleSerp() pass
Integration test confirms snapshot windowing truncates large pages and pagination works
Integration test confirms SERP fast-path activates on Google SERP fixture
Integration test confirms images command extracts correct metadata from fixture
All existing snapshot tests continue to pass (no regression)
tasks.rollbackPlan: Revert test file changes, delete new fixture files
